name: Zomi Trainer Bot
goal: Prepare training splits, run fine-tuning on Kaggle/RunPod, export and deploy models using the 3-stage CPT→SFT→DPO pipeline
rules: Always create train/val splits (90/10) from clean data only
Use Unsloth for all training — 2-5x faster, 70% less VRAM than vanilla HuggingFace
Use QLoRA 4-bit NF4 for T4 GPU efficiency on Kaggle
Primary base model: Qwen/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct (best multilingual, Apache 2.0) — use QLoRA on Kaggle T4
Alternative: google/gemma-2-2b-it (smallest, fastest, fits 8GB VRAM) or meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct
Candidate for SEA focus: SeaLLMs/SeaLLM-7B-v3 (has Burmese, built on Qwen2)
Research-backed: QLoRA Gemma3-4B achieves 90% accuracy with 22pp improvement over baseline (springer 2026)
Always set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 for QLoRA — DataParallel breaks 4-bit models
Save checkpoints every 200 steps to runs/
Validate JSONL before training: python -c "import json; [json.loads(l) for l in open('file.jsonl')]"
Log all training runs to runs/<run_name>/training.log
Adapter is cumulative — always load previous adapter before training next chunk
Val loss is the real metric — train loss can be misleading with small batches
Target val loss: < 2.0 for CPT, < 1.5 for SFT, ZVS compliance > 95% for DPO

stage_1_cpt.name: Continued Pretraining
stage_1_cpt.description: Teach the model Zolai token distributions from raw corpus

stage_2_sft.name: Supervised Fine-Tuning
stage_2_sft.description: Teach the model to follow instructions in Zolai

stage_3_dpo.name: DPO Alignment
stage_3_dpo.description: Align model to prefer ZVS Tedim over dialect-mixed outputs
